get.description: Get energy consumption data between a given time period.

parameters.name: hookId
parameters.description: Optional Hook ID. If omitted, the only matching hook on the extension is used; if the extension has multiple hooks of the relevant type, this becomes required.

parameters.name: meter_id
parameters.description: Meter ID for consumption data. Deprecated - use context_entities instead.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start date for consumption data (ISO 8601 format).

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End date for consumption data (ISO 8601 format).

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description: Interval between consumption data points (e.g., PT15M for 15 minutes, PT1H for hourly). Not all intervals have to be supported.

parameters.name: context_entities
parameters.description: Additional entities to include in the context for variable interpolation in the hook.

200.description: Consumption data returned successfully.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the consumption record.

type.description: Optional type of the consumption, such as 'nt' (night time) and 'ht' (high time) or 'feed-in' and 'feed-out'. Can be any string.

unit.description: Optional unit of the consumption value. Defaults to unit present on the relevant Meter Counter.

post.description: Asks an installed App to prepare a downloadable export of a visualization (consumption chart, dynamic tariff chart, etc.). The export is produced by the third-party App via a configured portal extension hook of type `dataExport` — this endpoint does not generate the file itself, it forwards the request to the configured hook and returns the descriptor the App provides (typically a `download_url`).

app_id.description: App ID providing the dataExport hook.

extension_id.description: Extension ID providing the dataExport hook.

hook_id.description: Optional Hook ID. If omitted, the only `dataExport` hook on the extension is used; if the extension has multiple `dataExport` hooks, this becomes required.

from.description: Optional start date for the export window (ISO 8601 format).

to.description: Optional end date for the export window (ISO 8601 format).

200.description: Export descriptor returned by the App.

download_url.description: URL the client can use to download the exported file. May be a pre-signed or short-lived URL.

filename.description: Suggested filename for the exported file.

content_type.description: MIME type of the exported file.

expires_at.description: Optional expiration timestamp for the download URL.

get.description: Returns runtime metadata describing how a visualization (consumption / price / cost chart) should be rendered for a given portal context (meter, contract, etc). Resolves the extension's `visualizationMetadata` hook implicitly from `app_id` + `extensionId` and invokes it. Supplies the response as a structured payload that the portal uses to configure type/aggregation options, supported intervals, and the available data range.

parameters.name: app_id
parameters.description: App ID providing the visualizationMetadata hook.

parameters.name: extensionId
parameters.description: Extension ID providing the visualizationMetadata hook.

parameters.name: context_entities
parameters.description: Entities to include in the context for variable interpolation in the hook (typically the meter and any other entities that scope the visualization).

200.description: Visualization metadata returned successfully.

post.description:
Walk every email-template config row in the caller's org and re-point any
field on `email_templates` that currently references `source_template_id`
at `destination_template_id`. Intended to be called from the email-template
migration flow when a duplicated template is refined.

Only v3-shaped rows are migrated (those carrying `portal_sk_v3`). Returns
the portal IDs that were rewritten and any whose update failed.

source_template_id.description: Template id currently referenced on portal rows

destination_template_id.description: Template id to write in place of the source

200.description: Migration completed (may have partial failures in failed_portal_ids).

post.description:
Read-only sibling of migrateEmailTemplateReferences. Lists every portal in
the caller's org whose `email_templates` config references `template_id`,
without rewriting anything. Used by the email-template MFE to show which
portals a template affects (in template settings and as a pre-migrate
preview). Uses the same discovery as the migrate path.

requestBody.description: Template id to look up references for

template_id.description: Email template id to find portal references for

200.description: Portals referencing the template.

name.description: Portal display name (or domain); falls back to id when unavailable

post.description:
**EXPERIMENTAL — do not rely on this endpoint.** It is unstable, currently limited to the `asset` schema, and may change or be removed without notice; third parties must not build on it yet.
Create a single entity on behalf of a portal user. The schema slug is passed in the path and must be one of the supported (experimental) schemas; field-level permissions are enforced by the caller's role grants. The request body is the entity to create (its attributes); the created entity is automatically related to the caller's contact.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Entity schema slug to create. Limited to the supported (experimental) schemas, currently `asset`.

201.description: The entity has been created successfully for the portal user.

patch.description:
**EXPERIMENTAL — do not rely on this endpoint.** It is unstable, currently limited to the `asset` schema, and may change or be removed without notice; third parties must not build on it yet.
Partially update a single entity on behalf of a portal user. The schema slug and entity id are passed in the path; the schema must be one of the supported (experimental) schemas. ExtensionHookContractIdentification.description:
Hook that replaces the built-in Contract identification for self-assignment. This hook involves an HTTP request whenever a user is trying to self-assign Contract(s).
The expected response http status code to the call is:
  - 200 if found
  - 404 if not found

The following assignment modes are supported:
  - `contracts`: We expect the response to contain Contract ids (customizable using `result` property).
  - `contact_to_contracts`: We expect the response to contain a Contact id (customizable using `result` property) and we will assign the Contact as a Customer to the Contracts and (optionally) update the Contact attribute specified by `contact_relation_attribute` to add the matched Contact.
  - `contact_to_portal_user`: We expect the response to contain a Contact id (customizable using `result` property) and we will assign the Contact to the Portal User. Portal User will be able to see all data including Contracts transitively.

Defaults to `contact_to_contracts` for backwards compatibility. We recommend using `contact_to_portal_user` as it does not influence the data model of business entities.

Hook that checks the plausibility of meter readings before they are saved. This hook makes a POST call whenever a user is trying to save a meter reading. The expected response to the call is:

    If meter reading is plausible, the response should contain:
      - valid: true
    If meter reading is not plausible, the response should contain:

Can be a string (counter ID) or an object with counter properties.
The backend resolves this to meter_counter_id in the final response.
Relevant only if plausibility_mode is "range".

ExtensionHookChangeEmail.description:
Hook that replaces the built-in change email functionality for portal users. When configured, the portal does not change the user's login email itself. Instead, this hook makes an HTTP call to the third-party system, which is expected to handle the email change (most likely by sending the user instructions to confirm the new email address).
The expected response http status code to the call is:
  - 2xx if the request was accepted
  - non-2xx if the request failed (optionally with a human-readable message resolved via `resolved.error_message_path`)

require_password_confirmation.description: Whether the portal user must confirm their current password before the change email request is handed over to the third-party system. When true, the portal collects and verifies the current password before calling the hook.

en.example: You will receive an email with instructions to confirm your new email address.

ExtensionHookDeleteAccount.description:
Hook that replaces the built-in delete account functionality for portal users. When configured, the portal does not delete the user itself. Instead, this hook makes an HTTP call to the third-party system, which is expected to handle the deletion.
The `deletion_mode` controls what the portal does after the call:
  - `synchronous`: The third-party system deletes the user immediately. The portal waits for a successful (2xx) response and then also deletes the epilot user.
  - `asynchronous`: The third-party system handles deletion out-of-band. The portal does not delete anything immediately; cleanup is expected to happen later (e.g. via the user deletion API or webhooks).

The expected response http status code to the call is:
  - 2xx if the request was accepted
  - non-2xx if the request failed (optionally with a human-readable message resolved via `resolved.error_message_path`)

deletion_mode.description: Controls how the account deletion is handled. `synchronous` waits for the third-party system to respond and then also deletes the epilot user. `asynchronous` hands the deletion over entirely to the third-party system and the portal does not delete anything immediately.

en.description: Explanation of the functionality shown to the end user.
en.example: Your account deletion will be processed by our system. This may take a few days.

ProviderConfig.description:
SSO identity provider configuration.

Env var interpolation: only string fields under `oidc_config` and
`mobile_oidc_config` (incl. their nested `metadata`) are passed through
Liquid templating, so they may contain `{{ env.VAR }}` placeholders that
get resolved at runtime against the organization's environment.

The following fields are used as literal values and MUST NOT contain
template syntax: `slug`, `display_name`, `provider_type`, all keys and
values under `attribute_mappings` (used as JSONPath-like accessors into
token claims), and all keys and values under `entity_matching`.

expose_client_secret.description:
Allow the resolved `client_secret` to be returned through the
public single-provider endpoint at SSO initiation. Only set this
for OIDC flows that require a public client secret in the
browser (e.g. some PKCE-less authorization-code variants). When
unset (default), the secret is kept server-side and only used at
the token exchange.

`ProviderConfig.display_name`) are NOT interpolated and must be literal.

Mobile OIDC configuration. Values are resolved at SSO invocation time, so the
fields below may reference org env vars via mustache-like templates, e.g.
`{{ env.MOBILE_CLIENT_SECRET }}`.

client_id.description: Client ID for the mobile app. Supports env var interpolation.

client_secret.description:
Client Secret for the mobile app. Supports env var interpolation (incl. secrets),
e.g. `{{ env.MOBILE_CLIENT_SECRET }}`.

response_modes_supported.description: Response modes the provider accepts (e.g. `form_post` for Apple)

logout_uri.description: URL of the logout/end session endpoint

logout_redirect_uri.description: URL to redirect to after logout completes

skip_login_as_logout.description: When true, skip SSO logout redirect during "login as" flow. Use this for providers that cannot redirect back after logout and would break log in as.

mobile_redirect_uri.description: URL of the mobile redirect URI

test_auth_username.description:
The username for the test auth, only used for testing on auth code flow.
Supports env var interpolation, e.g. `{{ env.MY_TEST_AUTH_USERNAME }}`.

test_auth_password.description:
The password for the test auth, only used for testing on auth code flow.
Supports env var interpolation (incl. secrets), e.g. `{{ env.MY_TEST_AUTH_PASSWORD }}`.

code.description: The code received from the external SSO provider

scope.description: The scope of the access token

visibility.description: The conditions that need to be met for the block to be shown

is_hidden.description: Block is fully hidden from portal users

hidden_on_mobile.description: Block is hidden on mobile viewports

hidden_on_desktop.description: Block is hidden on desktop viewports

hidden_in_app.description: Block is hidden in the mobile app

start_date.description: Schedule visibility start date. If only start_date is set, block is visible from this date onwards.

end_date.description: Schedule visibility end date. If only end_date is set, block is visible until this date.

visible_for_targets.description: Target IDs that must match for the block to be visible

hidden_for_targets.description: Target IDs that if matched will hide the block

BlockType.description: The type of the block. eg; tabs, tab, group, attribute

parentId.description: The id of the parent block

visibility.description: The conditions that need to be met for the page to be shown

is_hidden.description: Page is fully hidden from portal users

hidden_in_app.description: Page is hidden in the mobile app

start_date.description: Schedule visibility start date. If only start_date is set, page is visible from this date onwards.

end_date.description: Schedule visibility end date. If only end_date is set, page is visible until this date.

visible_for_targets.description: Target IDs that must match for the page to be visible

hidden_for_targets.description: Target IDs that if matched will hide the page

is_system.description: Whether the page is a system page

is_detail.description: Whether the page is a detail page

detail_schema.description: The schema of the detail page

show_in_navigation.description: Whether the detail page should appear in the main navigation

parentId.description: The id of the parent page

is_entry_route.description: Whether the page is the entry route

is_deleted.description: Send the flag as true to delete the page

last_modified_at.description: Last modified timestamp of the Page

past_routes.description: Slugs that previously belonged to this page. The portal redirects requests for these slugs to the current slug. Managed by the server: when a page's slug changes, the old slug is appended here, and when another page claims one of these slugs it is removed from this list.

mobile_config.description: Mobile app configuration (top-level; moved out of the config blob).

name.description: A short name to identify your portal

domain.description: The URL on which the portal is accessible

is_epilot_domain.description: Mark true if the domain is an Epilot domain

epilot_domain.description: The Epilot domain on which the portal is accessible

design_id.description: ID of the design used to build the portal

allowed_portal_entities.description: Allowed portal entities for the portal

self_registration_account_setting.description:
Controls behavior of self-registration when account is the registration
entity. `BLOCK_IF_PORTAL_USER_EXISTS` matches an existing account and
rejects the request when any portal user is already linked to that
account (no creation). Blocking can also be enabled on the other
non-create modes via `block_registration_if_portal_user_exists`.

block_registration_if_portal_user_exists.description:
Account-mode only. Reject registration when the resolved account already
has any portal user (any portal user whose mapped contact is linked to
the account).

self_registration_entity.description: Entity type used as the primary identifier for self-registration

user_account_self_management.description: Enable or disable user account self management

new_design.description: Enable or disable the new design for the portal

auto_redirect_to_sso.description: Decide whether to automatically redirect to the provider page during login, which would completely bypass showing the portal authentication page.

prevent_user_enumeration.description: Opt-in. When true, suppresses responses that reveal whether a user exists for public, pre-authentication actions (the login entry-point check and self-registration), at the expense of some UX. Already-authenticated actions are unaffected. Default false.

cognito_details.description: AWS Cognito Pool details for the portal

advanced_authentication.description: Advanced authentication settings for the portal

user_activity_logging.description: Enables detailed logging of user authentication attempts including risk assessments, IP addresses, user agents, and device information. These logs can be used for security analysis and monitoring.

adaptive_authentication.description: Automatically assesses risk for every authentication session. Based on risk ratings, can block authentication or require MFA for suspicious sign-in attempts. Helps protect user accounts from potential attacks by adapting security measures in real-time.

compromised_credentials_detection.description: Checks passwords against databases of leaked and commonly-guessed passwords during sign-up, sign-in, and password reset. Blocks or warns users when insecure passwords are detected, preventing unauthorized access from compromised credentials.

password_history_size.description: Number of previous passwords a user is prevented from reusing. Set to 0 to disable reuse prevention. Maps to Cognito's PasswordHistorySize and requires the user pool to be on the Essentials or Plus feature plan.

orderLeftTeaser.description: URL of the order left teaser image

orderRightTeaser.description: URL of the order right teaser image

welcomeBanner.description: URL of the welcome banner image

entity_identifiers.description: Identifiers used to identify an entity by a portal user. Deprecated. Use contract_identifiers instead.

attributes.description: Attributes used to identify an entity

contract_identifiers.description: Identifiers to identify a contract by a portal user.

contract_selector_config.description: Configuration for contract selector in the portal

show_inactive.description: Whether to show inactive contracts in the selector

title_path.description: Path to the property to use as the contract title

registration_identifiers.description: Identifiers to identify a contact of a portal user during the registration.

contact_identifiers_for_account.description:
Account-mode only. Identifiers on the contact entity of the primarily
identified account. Used to pick an existing related contact within the
resolved account; if none matches, the values are written onto the new
contact that is created and linked to the account.

additional_contact_attributes.description:
Contact attributes collected from the user during self-registration that are
written onto the newly created contact but are not used to identify an
existing one.

triggered_journeys.description: Journeys automatically opened on a portal user action

context_params.description:
Context parameters forwarded to the journey when it is
auto-triggered. Values may contain handlebars templates
that reference the available context (e.g.
`{{contact._id}}`, `{{portal_user.email}}`,
`{{order._id}}`) — these are resolved at trigger time by
`GET /v2/portal/config/triggered-journeys/{trigger_name}`
using the caller's auth context plus runtime entities
supplied via the `context_entities` query param.

entity_edit_rules.description: Rules for editing an entity by a portal user

meter_reading_grace_period.description: Grace period in days for meter readings

inactive_contract_cutoff_years.description: Number of years to look back for showing inactive contracts in the portal

is_dummy.description: Whether this is a dummy/test portal configuration

is_v3_item.description: Whether this is a v3 portal configuration

global_blocks.description: Portal-level blocks shared across all pages (e.g. footer). Keyed by block id.

PortalDataExportColumn.description: One column of the portal data export CSV.

key.description: Stable, immutable column id (snake_case). Append-only contract: never rename, reorder semantics, or remove an existing key; new columns are appended.

header.description: Localized header label, keyed by language (de, en).

source.description: Where the value comes from: relation hops from the anchor entity plus the attribute to read on the resolved entity. An empty path means the anchor itself.

path.description: Relation hops from the anchor entity (max 3).

attribute.description: Attribute to read on the resolved entity.

address_field.description: Which part of an address-typed attribute to render.

enum_labels.description: Localized value maps for the enum formatter, keyed by language then raw value.

enabled.description: Disable browser-side scripts that track advanced usage metrics

grants.description: Permissions granted to a portal user while accessing entities

is_dummy.description: Whether the org is in dummy mode

is_canary.description: Whether the org is in canary mode
